compiled_state.title: worktree sub-agent branch not accessible via git fetch origin after isolation commit
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: After a sub-agent completes work in an isolation worktree (isolation: worktree) and commits to its branch (e.g. worktree-agent-afacc1cd), the orchestrator cannot merge the results via 'git fetch origin <branch> && git merge'. The command fails with exit code 128 because the worktree branch is local to the agent's working directory and was never pushed to the remote. The per-worktree-review-commit workflow assumes the branch is fetchable from origin, but worktree isolation creates local-only branches. Workaround: use direct git operations against the worktree path rather than fetch+merge.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — dual-signal detected: (1) ticket describes agent behavior failure where workflow instructs fetch+merge on local-only branch; (2) affected file is prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md in prompts/ directory. Routing to bot-psychologist investigation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation complete (inline fallback — bot-psychologist sub-agent guard blocked nested dispatch). ROOT_CAUSE: Step 5 in per-worktree-review-commit.md used '<worktree-branch>' placeholder for git merge, which is ambiguous. Orchestrators can misinterpret this as requiring 'git fetch origin <branch>' before merging, which fails because worktree branches are local-only (never pushed to remote). Fix: Replace '<worktree-branch>' with a path-based SHA merge using WORKTREE_PATH (already set in Step 1): WORKTREE_HEAD=$(git -C "$WORKTREE_PATH" rev-parse HEAD) && git merge "$WORKTREE_HEAD" --no-edit. This is unambiguous, uses the already-defined WORKTREE_PATH variable, and explicitly notes that branches are local-only. taxonomy_item: Multi-Step Reasoning Drift (#7) — orchestrator has WORKTREE_PATH but Step 5 introduced a new branch-name variable causing drift toward fetch-from-origin. confidence: high. Test: tests/skills/test-per-worktree-merge-local-path.sh (5 assertions, all passing). Anti-pattern scan: no other fetch+merge-from-origin patterns found for worktree branches.
comments.author: Test
